steps.title: Identify the chronological signal
steps.content: Sentence 2 begins “When alive” and describes phytoplankton absorbing carbon. Sentence 3 begins with the blank and continues “after they die,” describing what happens next. The two sentences form a time-ordered sequence: alive $\Rightarrow$ dead. The blank must signal that the second event comes later in that sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each option:
  • Choice A — Specifically,: signals that what follows is a precise elaboration or narrowing of the previous idea. The death-and-sinking stage is not a specific detail within the living stage; it is a new, later stage. Violates the elaboration rule. ✗
  • Choice B — By contrast,: signals opposition. In both life and death phytoplankton contribute to carbon storage, so no genuine contrast exists. Violates the contrast rule. ✗
  • Choice C — Nevertheless,: signals concession — “despite what was just said, this is still true.” Nothing in sentence 2 creates an obstacle to sentence 3; the events simply happen in order. Violates the concession rule. ✗
  • Choice D — Then,: a temporal adverb signaling the next step in a sequence. It pairs perfectly with “When alive” by marking what happens afterward. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
